  3. Coaches

Coaches

A coach helps their lifter through the day, from weigh-in to the last attempt. Here is what a coach can and cannot do.

This is a plain-English guide. The official IPF Technical Rules are the final word.

The coaching area

During a lift, only the lifter, the spotters and the referees are on the platform. Coaches stay in the coaching area, set up so they can see the lift and signal to their lifter, for example about squat depth. This area is within about 5 metres of the stage.

How many coaches

At our competitions, you can have one coach or handler in the warm-up area. Sub-Junior lifters can have two. (Large international championships allow more, scaled to how many lifters a team has.)

What a coach does

  • Helps the lifter make weight and warm up
  • Chooses or agrees the lifter's attempts and passes them to the table
  • Makes sure the lifter's kit meets the rules at the equipment check
  • Gets the lifter to the platform ready and on time
  • Leaves the training and warm-up areas clean and tidy

Coach conduct

Coaches must follow anti-doping rules and keep to the dress code at bigger events. No hats, and no taking photos or filming from the coaching area. Children and family members are not allowed in the warm-up area. A coach who behaves badly can be warned, and then removed.

Next: safety and conduct
